title: Charmed Life - Chapter 15 (Fragment 4)
text:
And Gwendolen saw Cat and Janet. Cat knew she did, from the expression of surprise and faint annoyance on her face. Maybe she worked some magic of her own, or maybe the magic in the dragons‚Äô blood was simply used up. Whatever it was, the broken archway turned dark again, then milky, then to mist; and finally, there was nothing but meadow again between the pillars, and the air had stopped shimmering.

‚ÄúThat was Gwendolen,‚Äù Cat said.

‚ÄúI thought it was,‚Äù Janet said unappreciatively. ‚ÄúShe‚Äôll get fat if she has herself carried about like that all the time.‚Äù

‚ÄúShe was enjoying herself,‚Äù Cat said wistfully.

‚ÄúI could see that,‚Äù said Janet. ‚ÄúBut how do we find my world?‚Äù

Cat was not at all sure. ‚ÄúShall we try going around to the other side of the arch?‚Äù

‚ÄúSeems reasonable,‚Äù Janet agreed. She started to walk around the pillars, and stopped. ‚ÄúWe‚Äôd better get it right this time, Cat. You can only afford one more try. Or didn‚Äôt you lose a life on that one?‚Äù

‚ÄúI didn‚Äôt feel‚Äî‚Äù Cat began.

Then Mr. Nostrum was suddenly standing in the broken arch. He was holding the postcard Cat had sent to Mrs. Sharp, and he was cross and flustered.

‚ÄúMy dear boy,‚Äù he said to Cat, ‚ÄúI told you two-thirty, not midday. It was the merest chance that I had my hand on your signature. Let us hope all is not lost.‚Äù He turned and called over his shoul-der, apparently into the empty meadow, ‚ÄúCome on, William. The wretched boy seems to have misunderstood me, but the spell is clearly working. Don‚Äôt forget to bring the‚Äîah‚Äîequipment with you.‚Äù

He stepped out from between the pillars, and Cat backed away before him. Everything seemed to have gone very quiet. The leaves of the apple tree did not stir, and the small, small bubbling from the little spring changed to a soft, slow dripping. Cat had a strong suspicion that he and Janet had done something terrible. Janet was beyond the archway with her hands to her mouth, looking horrified. She was suddenly hidden by the large figure of Mr. William Nostrum, who popped into being from nowhere between the two pillars. He had a coil of rope around one arm, and there were shiny things sticking out of the pockets of his frock coat. His eyes were swiveling in an agitated way. He was a little out of breath.

‚ÄúPremature but successful, Henry,‚Äù he puffed. ‚ÄúThe rest have been summoned.‚Äù

William Nostrum stepped imposingly out beneath the apple tree beside his brother. The ground shook a little. The garden was quite silent. Cat backed away again and found that the little spring had stopped flowing. There was nothing but a muddy hole left. Cat was quite certain now that he and Janet had done something terrible.

Behind the Nostrums, other people came hurrying through the broken archway. The first one who came was one of the Accredited Witches from farther down Coven Street, puce in the face and very startled. She had been to church in her Sunday best: a monster of a hat with fruit and flowers in it, and a black and red satin dress. Most of the people who followed her were in Sunday best too: warlocks in blue serge and hard hats, witches in silk and bombazine and hats of all shapes and sizes, respectable-looking necromancers in frock coats like William Nostrum‚Äôs, skinny sorcerers in black, and quite a sprinkling of impressive wizards, who had either been to church in long black cloaks, or playing golf in very freckled plus fours. They came crowding between the pillars, first by twos and threes and then by sixes and sevens, all a little hasty and startled. Among them Cat recognized most of the witches and fortune-tellers from Coven Street, though he did not see either Mrs. Sharp or Miss Larkins‚Äîbut this may have
been simply that, in no time at all, he was being jostled this way and that in the middle of a large and steadily growing crowd.

vocabulary.definition: Feeling or appearing troubled or nervous.
vocabulary.example: eyes were swiveling in an agitated way

vocabulary.definition: A feeling of being slightly angry or irritated.
vocabulary.example: surprise and faint annoyance on her face

vocabulary.definition: An opening or passage framed by an arch.
vocabulary.example: the broken archway turned dark again

vocabulary.definition: A fabric made of silk and worsted, often black, used for mourning.
vocabulary.example: in silk and bombazine and hats of

vocabulary.definition: A loose outer garment that fastens at the neck and hangs loosely.
vocabulary.example: been to church in long black cloaks

vocabulary.definition: The look on someone's face that shows a particular emotion.
vocabulary.example: from the expression of surprise and faint

vocabulary.definition: Having small brownish spots on the skin.
vocabulary.example: playing golf in very freckled plus fours

vocabulary.definition: A man's long coat with a fitted waist and flared skirt.
vocabulary.example: of the pockets of his frock coat

vocabulary.definition: In a manner that impresses by size, appearance, or manner.
vocabulary.example: William Nostrum stepped imposingly out beneath the

vocabulary.definition: Evoking admiration through size, quality, or skill.
vocabulary.example: and quite a sprinkling of impressive wizards

vocabulary.definition: To push or bump against roughly, typically in a crowd.
vocabulary.example: he was being jostled this way and

vocabulary.definition: The use of special powers to make impossible things happen.
vocabulary.example: she worked some magic of her own

vocabulary.definition: Cloudy or opaque like milk.
vocabulary.example: then milky

vocabulary.definition: A light fog or thin cloud near the ground.
vocabulary.example: then to mist

vocabulary.definition: A sorcerer who communicates with the dead, often for magic or divination.
vocabulary.example: respectable-looking necromancers in frock coats like William

vocabulary.definition: Happening before the proper or expected time.
vocabulary.example: ‚ÄúPremature but successful

vocabulary.definition: A durable twilled woolen or worsted fabric.
vocabulary.example: warlocks in blue serge and hard hats

vocabulary.definition: To shine with a soft trembling or flickering light.
vocabulary.example: and the air had stopped shimmering.

vocabulary.definition: A person who practices sorcery or magic.
vocabulary.example: skinny sorcerers in black

vocabulary.definition: A small number or amount scattered about.
vocabulary.example: and quite a sprinkling of impressive wizards

vocabulary.definition: Achieving the desired aim or result.
vocabulary.example: ‚ÄúPremature but successful

vocabulary.definition: To order someone to come to a place.
vocabulary.example: ‚ÄúThe rest have been summoned.

vocabulary.definition: A sudden feeling of astonishment or shock.
vocabulary.example: the expression of surprise and faint annoyance

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows lack of gratitude or approval.
vocabulary.example: ‚Äù Janet said unappreciatively.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows a feeling of vague or regretful longing.
vocabulary.example: ‚Äù Cat said wistfully.
